name: Sam Prakash
headline: Velocity-Optimizing AI Builder-Operator | AI Product PMF Assessment | Agentic Systems Builder
target_role_types: Applied AI Lead (customer-outcome flavor, not solutions engineering)
Product Labs / Incubation Lead at a mature AI company
Chief of Staff to CPO / Head of AI (with explicit build scope)
positioning: Velocity-optimizing builder-operator targeting senior IC or first-level leader roles at mature AI-native companies. Best fit is work where the job-to-be-done is assessing AI product-market fit, reading customer/adoption signal, deciding the next hypothesis test, and rapidly prototyping or operationalizing it.
work_preferences: Strategic influence and proximity to senior product, AI, or GTM leadership
Creative autonomy to build prototypes and use working software as the strategy artifact
Fast learning loops: brainstorm early in the week, ship an improved prototype by the end of the week
Cultures where non-engineers are expected to build with AI, not only request work from technical teams
Performance-based scope expansion or promotion within roughly 18-24 months
role_fit_positive_signals: AI product-market fit assessment, product growth, usage/adoption signal analysis, customer-outcome sensemaking
Prototype-driven strategy, building internal AI tools, agents, automation, or new product workflows
Labs, incubation, new bets, 0-to-1 launches, greenfield systems, new function creation
Chief of Staff or strategic projects roles with explicit build scope, not calendar/process ownership
Applied AI roles focused on customer outcomes and product learning, not solutions engineering
role_fit_negative_signals: Engineering, solutions architect, sales engineer, customer engineer, forward deployed engineer, ML engineer, or research engineer titles
Pure planning operations, BDR operations, territory/quota operations, reporting cadence ownership, or dashboard delivery
Relationship-only partnerships, account management, customer success operations, onboarding, or enablement roles
Roles where the main output is executive decks, stakeholder consensus, or scaling an already-settled operating model
Slow enterprise ceremony where every prototype requires heavy approval

constraints.location: Open to SF or NYC; Seattle/Renton base
constraints.compensation: Target total compensation floor is approximately $300K; verify cash vs. equity-heavy private-company offers
constraints.career_horizon: Optimize for 18-24 months of maximum learning and strategic outcome density

differentiators: Built production AI systems end-to-end (GenAI-Intel, Forge, Job Search Intel, AWS internal agents)
Lead a 7-person cross-functional AI pillar (Proactive Intelligence) across the AWS Startups org
Builds the ideal state on the frontier (Claude) and replicates within Amazon's tooling constraints, deliberately mapping what the jagged frontier enables
Pioneered Primary AI Provider (PAIP) as a forward-looking companion to PCP, surfacing the neocloud/GPU shift before it shows up as a startup's primary cloud
12+ years total experience, 6.5+ years at AWS Startups
Rare fluency across business strategy, AI infrastructure, and hands-on product building
Live deployed products as proof, not just claims
experience_summary: 12+ years of total experience, including 6.5+ years at AWS Startups driving revenue performance and executive decision systems at scale, with a rare combination of GTM strategy, AI product-market-fit sensemaking, and hands-on AI systems building. Built multiple production AI systems including GenAI-Intel, Forge, Job Search Intel, and internal AWS agentic tooling; strongest when using prototypes and data products to help senior leaders decide what to build next. Currently leads the Proactive Intelligence Pillar, one of six AI pillars driven across the AWS Startups segment.
summary: Velocity-optimizing AI builder-operator with 12+ years of total experience, including 6.5+ years at AWS Startups driving revenue performance and executive decision systems at scale. Strongest at the intersection of AI product strategy, GTM signal, and rapid prototyping: turning ambiguous customer/adoption signals into working systems that help leaders assess product-market fit and choose the next build. Architected agentic intelligence systems that moved executive workflows from manual analysis and reporting into autonomous, insight-driven cadences. Currently leads the Proactive Intelligence Pillar, one of six AI pillars driven across the AWS Startups segment.
education: MS Business Analytics, W.P. Carey / ASU; MBA, IIM Raipur; B.Tech Civil Engineering, BPUT

projects.title: Cloud-Intel
projects.subtitle: Automated Intelligence System
projects.description: Built to automate what I was doing manually at AWS - identifying which VC-funded startups are running on which cloud and AI providers and prioritizing which ones to engage. A live AI intelligence platform tracking 200+ VC-funded startups with cloud and AI provider attribution, vertical classification, and engagement prioritization. Core: a 4-tier attribution engine (deterministic signals → strong inference → supporting signals → dual LLM fallback via Perplexity Sonar + Claude Haiku) with confidence scoring and entrenchment ratings. Scans DNS records, subprocessor/DPA pages, job postings across 15 VC job boards, partnership announcements, technical docs, privacy policies, IP/ASN ranges, and tech blogs - ~2,500 automated web searches per daily pipeline run. Phase 1 added vertical/sub-vertical classification across 84 sub-verticals with cloud propensity tags (High/Medium/Low). Phase 2 added engagement priority tiers (Engage Now / Watch / Track), trigger detection (hiring surges, leadership changes, product launches, press features, vendor partnerships), and LLM-generated outreach intelligence with a dedicated “Ready to Engage” view. Phase 3 added API cost observability - granular per-function search usage tracking with a stacked bar chart and KPI cards on the dashboard. Architected and shipped end to end with AI-assisted development (Claude Code).
projects.stack: FastAPI, Python, Next.js, Supabase, Claude API (Anthropic), Claude Code, Perplexity Sonar, Serper.dev (Google Search), Recharts, Railway, Vercel
projects.outcome: Live at cloud-intel.vercel.app. Daily automated pipeline: 167+ startups tracked with cloud/AI attribution, vertical classification, propensity tags, engagement tiers, trigger detection, LLM-generated outreach intelligence, and API cost monitoring. Full product across 6 dashboard views.

projects.title: Forge
projects.subtitle: Communication & Interview Practice OS
projects.description: Built to systematize professional communication and interview preparation the way athletes train - structured reps, AI scoring, and progress tracking instead of ad-hoc practice. A live Next.js practice OS with two daily loops: (1) writing prompts scored by Claude across clarity, structure, concision, and persuasion, and (2) speaking practice with browser audio recording, OpenAI Whisper transcription, and Claude-based delivery feedback. Includes an interview-prep mode that imports company/role-specific prompt packs from Job Search Intel through a secured server-to-server API, organizes them in a prompt library, and runs full mock interview rounds with multi-question recording, transcript capture, holistic readiness verdicts, per-question scoring, strengths, and improvement areas. Tracks streaks, recent reps, skill radar, score history, coaching notes, and progress analytics by company and role. Architected and shipped end to end with AI-assisted development (Claude Code).
projects.stack: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Supabase Auth/Postgres/RLS, Claude API (Anthropic), OpenAI Whisper, Vercel
projects.outcome: Live at forge-pi-livid.vercel.app. Integrated with Job Search Intel as the Phase 2 interview-practice handoff target: scored roles can generate Forge-ready company/role prompt packs, which Forge imports into mock interviews and progress analytics for targeted preparation.

projects.title: Job Search Intel
projects.subtitle: AI-Powered Job Search Platform
projects.description: Built to replace the manual cycle of checking career pages, reading JDs, and guessing which roles are worth applying to. A live job search intelligence platform monitoring 25 target companies across model providers, big tech, GTM/revenue intelligence, and AI-native startups. Core: automated role discovery via Brave Search across Greenhouse, Lever, and Ashby with JD scraping and deduplication. Claude API scores each role across 5 dimensions (domain fit, technical fit, seniority fit, role type fit, H1B likelihood) with a realism filter and match tiers (Strong / Worth Applying / Stretch / Skip). Resume tailoring engine analyzes each JD against full structured resume data and generates grounded prioritization - section reordering, bullet priorities, keyword optimization, and a tailored summary - with a constraint that prevents fabricating expertise not evidenced in the actual resume. Generates downloadable .docx resumes per role via a Node.js document builder with fuzzy bullet matching. Includes interview intel gathering from Reddit and Levels.fyi, daily automated discovery via Vercel Cron with email digest via Resend, and session handoff to Forge for targeted practice. Architected and shipped end to end with AI-assisted development (Claude Code).
projects.stack: FastAPI, Python, Next.js 16, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Supabase, Claude API (Anthropic), Brave Search API, Node.js, Resend, Railway, Vercel
projects.outcome: Live at job-search-intel.vercel.app. Daily automated pipeline: 25 companies monitored, roles discovered and deduplicated, 5-dimension match scoring, grounded resume tailoring with .docx generation, interview intel, and email digest notifications.

professional_ai_projects.title: AI Executive Reporting Toolkit - Startup Pulse
professional_ai_projects.description: Architected the multi-agent AI system behind Startup Pulse, a CEO-facing reporting program. A coordinated agent pipeline handles data gathering, consolidation, review, and formatting, assembling a 360-degree view across internal metrics, CRM engagement, external funding signals, customer snapshots, and cloud/AI provider inference. It tracks 2000+ strategic startups and powers three CEO-facing reporting cadences: a monthly top-50 funded list, a quarterly top-1000, and a top-500 report for the Bi-Weekly Business Review (BWBR) reviewed by Andy Jassy and his direct reports.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Amazon Kiro (Opus 4.7 backend), Amazon Quick (frontend), PizzaBot, Salesforce MCP integration
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Reduced end-to-end report production from 6 weeks to under a week, a 70% reduction in report generation time, recovering 600 hours per cycle across three individuals and enabling consistent, scalable executive delivery without analyst bottlenecks. Cited by Andy Jassy in Amazon's Q1 2026 earnings call.

professional_ai_projects.title: Investor Pulse
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Agentic System
professional_ai_projects.description: Built an end-to-end agentic pipeline to shift VC relationship management from anecdotal to analytical - crawling top-tier and competitor-aligned VC funds (Sequoia, a16z, M12, GV, CapitalG) to extract structured portfolio metadata (domain, investment year, acquisition/IPO status), enriched via 1P/3P data agents with funding and usage signals. A filtering layer scopes the universe to active, recently funded startups (3-year lookback), while a PCP inference agent identifies Primary Cloud Provider across the portfolio and surfaces white space opportunities and joint-success signals by VC, stage, and vintage. A notification agent drives accountable follow-through across Startup Investment Managers and Account teams, with structured output piped directly into Salesforce and Outlook workflows.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Claude (via PizzaBot AI), MCP-integrated tooling across Salesforce, Outlook, and local filesystem
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Delivered a portfolio intelligence layer spanning 15+ VC funds, equipping Investment Manager teams to enter VC conversations armed with PCP penetration metrics, white space signals, and stage-specific engagement prioritization, shifting from relationship intuition to repeatable, data-driven pipeline development. Packaged as the VC-intelligence briefing for Andy Jassy's office.

professional_ai_projects.title: Competitor Pulse
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Agentic System
professional_ai_projects.description: Built to replace scattered field observations with a structured monthly intelligence cadence on emerging cloud threats - a scheduled GenAI-powered agent that synthesizes internal CRM signals (field notes, deal outcomes, customer conversations) with external market data (product launches, funding rounds, market commentary) to produce a monthly competitive threat assessment targeting GPU-specialized clouds (CoreWeave), neo-clouds (Nebius), sovereign clouds, and emerging developer tools across the broadly evolving cloud competitive space. Each report surfaces at-risk accounts, evaluates current account team positioning, and recommends mitigation strategies - with a built-in accountability loop tracking execution against prior-quarter recommendations.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Claude (scheduled agent), MCP-integrated tooling across CRM and Email
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Delivered monthly structured threat reports to the Startup Sales Operations Director and as the competitive-intelligence briefing for Andy Jassy's office, closing the gap between competitive reality and field perception, with a quarter-over-quarter accountability loop ensuring recommended counter-positioning actions translate to measurable field follow-through.

professional_ai_projects.title: Loss Signal Validator
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Agentic System
professional_ai_projects.description: Built to cut through CRM noise and surface the real drivers behind deal outcomes - an agentic workflow that reads and analyzes Salesforce loss reasoning at scale, cross-referencing field-entered rationale against first-party engagement data and external evidence (press releases, customer announcements, third-party reports) to validate or challenge sales team assertions. Filters out subjective and inconsistent inputs to surface high-signal patterns across specific deal drivers and needle-moving workloads. Monthly outputs maintain ongoing win/loss theme visibility; quarterly deep-dives deliver verified strategic recommendations with a built-in accountability loop tracking execution against prior interventions.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Claude (agentic workflow), MCP-integrated across Salesforce and 1P data
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Replaced subjective CRM reporting with fact-checked win/loss intelligence, surfacing verified deal drivers, validated competitive loss patterns, and quarterly strategic recommendations with quarter-over-quarter progress tracking against prior interventions.

professional_ai_projects.title: PCP Reference App
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Web Application
professional_ai_projects.description: A leadership-facing web application that returns the inferred Primary Cloud Provider and Primary AI Provider (PCP/PAIP) for any startup on demand. It synthesizes signals across external sources and AWS internal data to infer both, surface competitive context (including neocloud and GPU-provider usage), and feed directly into startup strategy. Built for the leadership team first, with rollout to the wider Sales org underway.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Amazon Kiro (Opus 4.7 backend), Amazon Quick (frontend), AWS MCPs
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Live leadership tool for on-demand PCP lookup; expanding to the wider AWS Startups Sales org within a month.

professional_ai_projects.title: Startup 360 Knowledge Graph
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Semantic Profiles
professional_ai_projects.description: Knowledge-graph-based Startup 360 profiles for each top startup in the reporting pipeline, enabling semantic natural-language search across the portfolio. Turns structured and inferred startup attributes (funding, investor, PCP, vertical, timing) into a queryable graph, so a question like 'which a16z-funded startups in the last 6 months are primarily on AWS?' resolves without manual filtering. Distinct from the 2021 Startup 360 seller mechanism.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Knowledge graph, Claude (semantic query), AWS internal data
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Natural-language semantic search over top-startup profiles, replacing manual filtering with graph queries.

professional_ai_projects.title: Proactive Intelligence Engine (Claude-side prototype)
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Agentic System - dual build
professional_ai_projects.description: The frontier reference build of the Proactive Intelligence pillar, built on Claude to define the ideal state, then replicated within Amazon's internal tooling. A watcher (signal detection) feeds a classifier and an account-manager writer agent, with knowledge-graph memory for net-new detection, a self-improving skill loop that proposes prompt revisions for human approval, and a frozen-fixture eval harness that uses bidirectional pairwise judging to separate real quality wins from noise. Runs on Claude Code, designed to migrate to Bedrock AgentCore.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Claude (Sonnet + Haiku), Perplexity Sonar, knowledge-graph memory, Supabase, frozen-fixture eval harness
professional_ai_projects.outcome: A working frontier prototype that maps what the evolving model frontier enables, then transfers those patterns into Amazon's constrained tooling to maximize value for leadership stakeholders.

professional_ai_projects.title: PCP-Intel: Attribution Accuracy Auto-Optimization Loop
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Agentic System - In Design
professional_ai_projects.description: Designed to eliminate manual signal weight tuning in the GenAI-Intel cloud attribution pipeline - replacing ad-hoc threshold adjustments with an autonomous overnight optimization loop modeled on Karpathy's AutoResearch pattern. A validation agent runs the attribution pipeline against a curated, human-verified ground truth eval set of 30-50 startups stratified across confidence tiers, PCP categories, and known edge cases (multi-cloud, conflicting signals, false positive-prone patterns). An optimization agent reads the current signal weight config and a natural-language research brief, forms a hypothesis, proposes a targeted weight adjustment across five attribution signals (partnership pages, subprocessor lists, DNS records, marketplace listings, job postings), and re-scores against a composite accuracy metric that penalizes false positives more heavily than misses. A logging agent persists each experiment - hypothesis, config delta, score delta, keep/revert decision - to Supabase, building an auditable history of what the pipeline learned. Runs ~12 experiments per hour unattended, surfacing the best-performing config each morning.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Claude (multi-agent orchestration), MCP (Supabase, Filesystem), Brave Search
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Designed to replace manual attribution tuning with a closed-loop, evidence-driven optimization system - continuously improving PCP signal accuracy overnight and surfacing a validated, experiment-backed config ready for production promotion each morning.

professional_ai_projects.title: LastMile: Seller Intelligence Collection Engine
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Agentic System - In Design
professional_ai_projects.description: Designed to eliminate the manual follow-up burden of collecting last-mile PCP and AI provider data from sellers when automated attribution signals are exhausted - a 5-agent orchestration that closes the data gap in executive reporting pipelines. A resolution agent maps each unattributed startup domain to its Salesforce account and identifies the assigned seller; an outreach agent composes and sends a contextualized data request with account-specific context pre-populated. A reply monitoring agent reads inbound responses and classifies completion state (data provided, committed timeline, needs more time, no reply). A follow-up scheduling agent sends aptly timed reminders based on the seller's stated timeline rather than a generic cadence. A notification agent surfaces unresolved cases to operations for escalation, with structured output feeding directly back into the reporting pipeline.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Claude (multi-agent orchestration), MCP-integrated across Salesforce and Email
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Designed to replace manual seller follow-up with a closed-loop, context-aware collection system - ensuring last-mile PCP and AI provider data reaches executive reports on time without operations manually chasing individual account teams.

professional_ai_projects.title: ActionWire: GTM Trigger-to-Action Engine
professional_ai_projects.subtitle: Agentic System - In Design
professional_ai_projects.description: Designed to close the gap between market signals and seller action - a multi-agent orchestration that monitors for high-intent startup triggers (funding rounds, product launches, leadership changes) and drives a fully automated assess-then-act workflow. A signal detection agent identifies and validates the trigger; an account assessment agent pulls a 360-degree view of the account across CRM history, engagement depth, deal stage, and owner status. A routing agent branches based on account state: managed accounts surface a contextualized alert and recommended action directly to the assigned seller; unmanaged accounts bypass the seller entirely and trigger a contextually grounded outreach email to the startup contact. All actions are logged and tracked for follow-through.
professional_ai_projects.stack: Claude (multi-agent orchestration), MCP-integrated across Salesforce, Email, and external funding data sources (Crunchbase / Brave Search)
professional_ai_projects.outcome: Designed to eliminate signal-to-action latency in startup GTM coverage - ensuring every fundable trigger reaches either a seller or a contact within hours, not weeks, with context-aware routing that respects account ownership boundaries.

work_history.title: Sr. GTM Sales Operations Manager, Startups
bullets: Lead the Proactive Intelligence Pillar, one of six AI pillars across the AWS Startups segment, directing a cross-functional team of 7 to move beyond prompt-writing into agentic architecture - automating existing workflows and building new ones that serve Sales, Investment Managers, and Leadership across the org.

Drove Primary Cloud Provider (PCP) from an obscure data point to the most-tracked metric in the Startup organization - now a front-and-center business metric mandated from Andy Jassy's office and adopted across AWS leadership, enabled by the AI scale-up delivered over the past year.
Annual performance rating of Exceeds High Bar for two consecutive years.

Pioneered Primary AI Provider (PAIP) as a companion metric to PCP: PCP data showed little evidence of startups choosing neoclouds, so I expanded the lens to capture who startups use for AI workloads, which surfaced neoclouds and GPU providers. PAIP is forward-looking - as cloud spend shifts to GPUs, it flags rising GPU workloads on a neocloud before it becomes a startup's primary cloud. This seeded dedicated competitive-intelligence reporting that synthesizes external public signals with internal CRM data for a holistic read on the evolving competitive landscape and our response.
Enabled the Startup Operations team on AI tools.

Designed and operationalized performance measurement framework for market share tracking; elevated to key organizational metric monitored at executive level, directly influencing senior leadership goal-setting and strategic planning cycles.

Architected agentic AI-powered automation & augmentation solutions for executive reporting workflows, streamlining complex analytical processes and improving data quality across leadership reporting cadences.

Led product definition and cross-functional development of Startup Pulse - AWS’s first AI-powered PCP and primary AI provider identification system, now in productionization with Data and Software Engineering. Achieved 10x scale improvement in analysis capacity through GenAI automation; saves 70% of report generation time.
Applied the Startup Pulse prototype to generate the top startups list for AWS re:Invent, used directly by AWS CEO Matt Garman.
Launched and owned the Monthly Top Startups Report for Matt Garman’s office (FY’24) - developed ranking logic across 7 cohorts covering 175 high-potential startups.
Delivered the Top 100 AI Startups report for Andy Jassy’s office - developed and refined the ranking, coordinated with 130+ account teams.

Led strategic planning workstream for executive business review cycles - developed performance dashboards for leadership visibility and conducted comprehensive performance analysis across organizational priorities.
Originated and socialized the strategic framework that vertical classification - not funding size alone - should be the primary filter for identifying high-potential startups.
Led cross-functional data integration initiatives to unlock new internal use cases, evangelizing GenAI adoption beyond dashboards to the full leadership org.
Collaborated cross-functionally with Finance teams to design data-driven allocation frameworks.

Built a cross-functional team of 12 and pioneered use of primary and secondary research for capital investment signal tracking, driving 200%+ increase in leads and 182% growth in client inquiries, expanding the qualified pipeline to $250M+.
